---
title: "Art & Music"
page-layout: full
---

```{=html}
<div class="page-header">
  <h1>Art &amp; Music</h1>
  <p class="subtitle"><a href="https://www.instagram.com/mulberryandelm" target="_blank">@mulberryandelm &nearr;</a></p>
</div>
<!-- 
<div class="art-intro">
  <p>
    Tufte argued that good data visualisation is an aesthetic act. I&rsquo;d add that good
    ceramics requires the same systematic thinking as a benchmarking framework. These aren&rsquo;t
    hobbies separate from my science &mdash; they&rsquo;re the same impulse: find the form that
    makes the invisible legible.
  </p>
</div> -->

<div class="section-divider">&bull;</div>

<!-- PAINTING -->
<div class="art-section">
  <h2>Painting</h2>
  <!-- <p class="art-meta">22nd UOB Painting of the Year Exhibition &bull; Singapore &bull; 2003</p> -->
  <!-- <p>
    Oils and acrylics. Drawn to atmosphere, gesture, and the moment before a form resolves
    &mdash; the suspended instant where something is almost but not yet identifiable.
    The same interest in thresholds that shapes my behavioural science.
  </p> -->
  <div class="gallery-grid">
    <div class="gallery-item">
      <a href="images/paintings/sunset-trees.jpg" target="_blank" class="gallery-link">
      <img src="images/paintings/sunset-trees.jpg" alt="Acrylics on canvas 2004" loading="lazy">
      </a>
      <div class="gallery-caption">Acrylic on canvas, 2004.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="gallery-item">
      <a href="images/paintings/japanese-garden.jpg" target="_blank" class="gallery-link">
      <img src="images/paintings/japanese-garden.jpg" alt="Japanese garden plein air" loading="lazy">
      </a>
      <div class="gallery-caption">Japanese garden. Acrylic on canvas, plein air, 2019.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="gallery-item">
      <a href="images/paintings/impasto-city.jpg" target="_blank" class="gallery-link">
      <img src="images/paintings/impasto-city.jpg" alt="Thick palette-knife impasto mountain scape at sunrise — yellow sky, dark blue-purple buildings" loading="lazy">
      </a>
      <div class="gallery-caption">黄山. Acrylic on canvas, palette knife, 2019.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="gallery-item">
      <a href="images/paintings/son-in-field.jpg" target="_blank" class="gallery-link">
      <img src="images/paintings/son-in-field.jpg" alt="Small figure in red walking across a green sunlit field — oils on canvas" loading="lazy">
      </a>
      <div class="gallery-caption">Small child in red running into a hedge. Acrylics on canvas, 2025.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="gallery-item">
      <a href="images/paintings/portrait.jpg" target="_blank" class="gallery-link">
      <img src="images/paintings/portrait.jpg" alt="Portrait in oils — face lit in yellow-green against deep blue clothing" loading="lazy">
      </a>
      <div class="gallery-caption">Self portrait. Acrylic on canvas, 2018.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="gallery-item">
      <a href="images/paintings/coastal-plein-air.jpg" target="_blank" class="gallery-link">
      <img src="images/paintings/coastal-plein-air.jpg" alt="Wide coastal bay plein air — muted grey sky, teal water, warm sand foreground" loading="lazy">
      </a>
      <div class="gallery-caption">Juara beach. Acrylic on canvas, plein air, 2018.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="gallery-item">
      <a href="images/paintings/angkor-temple.jpg" target="_blank" class="gallery-link">
      <img src="images/paintings/angkor-temple.jpg" alt="Ta Prohm temple, Angkor — coloured pencil, tree roots engulfing stone" loading="lazy">
      </a>
      <div class="gallery-caption">Ta Prohm, Angkor. Coloured pencil, 2018.</div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="section-divider">&bull;</div>

<!-- CERAMICS -->
<div class="art-section">
  <h2>Ceramics</h2>
  <!-- <p class="art-meta">The Art of the Cup &bull; Cambridge, MA, USA &bull; 2014</p> -->
  <!-- <p>
    Functional vessels. Wheel-thrown stoneware, with interest in the conversation between
    thrown form and surface treatment &mdash; where the physics of clay meeting resistance
    produces an outcome that is partly designed and partly discovered.
    That negotiation is the interesting part.
  </p> -->
  <div class="gallery-grid">
    <div class="gallery-item">
      <a href="images/ceramics/curl-handle-mug.jpg" target="_blank" class="gallery-link">
      <img src="images/ceramics/curl-handle-mug.jpg" alt="Cream stoneware mug with sculpted curling handle that ends in a tight spiral" loading="lazy">
      </a>
      <div class="gallery-caption">Mug.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="gallery-item">
      <a href="images/ceramics/herringbone-pitcher.jpg" target="_blank" class="gallery-link">
      <img src="images/ceramics/herringbone-pitcher.jpg" alt="Amber-glazed pitcher with elegant long neck — lower body covered in dense herringbone impressed texture" loading="lazy">
      </a>
      <div class="gallery-caption">Herringbone pitcher.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="gallery-item">
      <a href="images/ceramics/spiral-plate.jpg" target="_blank" class="gallery-link">
      <img src="images/ceramics/spiral-plate.jpg" alt="Celadon plate viewed from above — concentric thrown rings at centre, carved radiating leaf-marks filling the field" loading="lazy">
      </a>
      <div class="gallery-caption">Spiral plate.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="gallery-item">
      <a href="images/ceramics/marbled-bowl-interior.jpg" target="_blank" class="gallery-link">
      <img src="images/ceramics/marbled-bowl-interior.jpg" alt="Nerikomi bowl viewed from directly above — black and white marbled clay spirals inward to a vortex" loading="lazy">
      </a>
      <div class="gallery-caption">Marbled bowl.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="gallery-item">
      <a href="images/ceramics/sea-urchin-vessel.jpg" target="_blank" class="gallery-link">
      <img src="images/ceramics/sea-urchin-vessel.jpg" alt="Small spherical vessel with barnacle-like textured surface, cobalt blue glaze pooling in the crevices" loading="lazy">
      </a>
      <div class="gallery-caption">Vessel.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="gallery-item">
      <a href="images/ceramics/teal-nerikomi-yunomi.jpg" target="_blank" class="gallery-link">
      <img src="images/ceramics/teal-nerikomi-yunomi.jpg" alt="Tall yunomi with teal glaze over nerikomi marbled body — dark slip waves rising from the base" loading="lazy">
      </a>
      <div class="gallery-caption">Cup.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="gallery-item">
      <a href="images/ceramics/blue-stripe-mug.jpg" target="_blank" class="gallery-link">
      <img src="images/ceramics/blue-stripe-mug.jpg" alt="Round belly mug with combed blue and white slip — feathered horizontal stripes in ink-blue and cream" loading="lazy">
      </a>
      <div class="gallery-caption">Cup.</div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="section-divider">&bull;</div>

<!-- MUSIC -->
<div class="art-section">
  <h2>Music</h2>
  <p class="art-meta">Classical Soprano: art songs, a cappella</p>

  <div class="music-block">
    <p class="award-label">Gold Medal</p>
    <h3>17th International Festival of Advent and Christmas Music</h3>
    <p>Raffles Concert Singers, Prague, Czech Republic &bull; 2007</p>
  </div>

  <p style="margin-top: 1.5rem;">
    Studied voice at UC San Diego (Minor: Music).
    Solo repertoire centred on French impressionists: Poulenc, Debussy, Faur&eacute;; opera excerpts.
    Group repertoire centred on Renaissance and modern/post-modern a cappella music.
  </p>
  <!-- <p>
    The discipline of singing &mdash; breath, phrase, the physics of resonance &mdash; shares
    more with experimental design than it might seem. Both require understanding what the
    system will and will not do, and working with rather than against its constraints.
  </p> -->
</div>

<div class="section-divider">&bull;</div>

<!-- TUFTE NOTE -->
<!-- <div class="art-section" style="max-width: 720px; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;">
  <div class="tufte-note">
    <p>
      The whorlmap visualisation I designed for DABEST is both a statistical tool and a
      design object. Every aesthetic decision &mdash; colour, spacing, the choice to show
      the bootstrap distribution as a half-violin rather than error bars &mdash; was made
      to communicate uncertainty honestly and beautifully.
    </p>
    <p>
      That is not a coincidence. It is the same skill as deciding where the glaze should
      pool on a thrown form, or how long to hold a phrase before resolving it.
      The question in each case is: what is the minimum you need to show, and how do you
      show it so clearly that nothing else needs to be said?
    </p>
  </div>
</div> -->
```
